integration: body-keyed MoE routing (router sees ONLY the live body vector) + NTL dual-key + expert diversity
honest_scope: Router keyed by live body only; wrong key routes every inserted layer's experts wrong. NTL makes wrong-key destructive. Patch test isolates routing as the causal locus (double dissociation). A LEARNED router memorizes its (die,nonce) key, so a different nonce also fails (key-specific) -- cross-nonce FRESHNESS is delegated to the deterministic keystream layer, not claimed here. Physics generic; per-die uniqueness from prefcore fingerprint.
verdict: MoE GREEN (load-bearing + per-die, at trained nonce): a frozen GPT-2's per-layer expert routing is load-bearing on the body key; foreign-die/random key -> high PPL; patching native routing into a foreign-key run recovers PPL (routing is the causal locus). Freshness delegated to keystream layer.
